title: Analysis of choroid thickness on OCT image using MATLAB
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e9ln7","text":"This protocol describes how to use a custom written MATLAB script to analyze thickness of choroid on OCT images of both eyes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Make sure you have MATLAB 2016b (or newer) with image processing toolbox installed on your computer.

Run OCT_file_processing.m to process OCT images. This script works on raw image generated by Optovue OCT machines. It will make the file name easier to handle and extract image content from cross line scans and line scans and save them into tiff format.
Run OCT_ImageAnalysis_for_CrossLine.m through all the tif files.

Follow the prompt and draw along the contour of the retina on the left side of the image. a Then follow the prompt and draw along the contour of the choroid on the left side of the image. a After you're done, choroid thickness will be calculated for you. Red lines represent auxiliary lines perpendicular to the curve. Green lines represent calculated thickness of the curve. a Repeat this process for the right side of the image. Another figure will appear and summarize the thickness measurement. a

After running the script for all four cross lines for both eyes. Run OCT_Data_Comparison.m to generate interpolated data and compare restuls between two eyes. Choroid fhickness data in eight corners will be plotted and an interpolated picture will be shown. An xls file summarizing statistics will also be generated. a

On the Optovue OCT machine, export cross line OCT images. To allow inperpolation, four cross line images are required for each eye. Single cross line image can still be processed by OCT_ImageAnalysis_for_CrossLine.m.
creator: Chao-Yuan Yeh
